topic: Relational & event-log features: joins, aggregations, and point-in-time correctness
scope: Extracting features from relational tables and event logs. Covers one-to-many joins, aggregating event histories into entity-level features, the risk of fan-out duplication, and the critical importance of point-in-time correctness (as-of joins) to prevent data leakage.
key_concepts: Most real-world data resides in relational databases, requiring joins across multiple tables (one-to-many or many-to-many) to construct a flat feature matrix.
Careless joins can cause fan-out duplication, where the target entity is replicated multiple times, distorting the dataset.
Event-log features summarize historical actions (e.g., clicks, purchases) into static entity-level metrics (e.g., total purchases in the last 7 days).
Point-in-time correctness is mandatory: when aggregating past events, we must only include events that occurred strictly before the prediction timestamp.
Failing to enforce point-in-time constraints leads to severe data leakage, where future information contaminates the historical feature.

definitions.definition: The unintentional duplication of rows in a primary table caused by joining it with a secondary table that has multiple matching records.
The guarantee that features computed for a specific moment in time rely only on information available prior to that moment.
A relational join that matches a record to the most recent preceding record in another table based on a timestamp.

examples.description: Aggregating a user's purchase history into a single feature "purchases_last_30_days", ensuring that if the prediction is for June 1st, purchases on June 2nd are not counted.

relations.builds_toward: Automated Feature Engineering (Deep Feature Synthesis) in Chapter 16.
relations.prerequisites: Group-based aggregations from ch06_s03. The concept of target availability cutoff from Ch 1.

figures.caption: Mekanisme As-Of Join untuk Menghindari Kebocoran Data
figures.description: Diagram yang membandingkan join standar (yang tidak sengaja memasukkan kejadian dari masa depan) dengan as-of join yang secara ketat memutus agregasi tepat sebelum waktu prediksi.

exclusions: Detailed SQL syntax or pandas syntax for as-of joins (focus on the concept).
